Details: One study showed that the inactivation of B. anthracis spores in water by ultraviolet irradiation (UV) can be effective. The lethal dose to inactivate 90% (1 log) of spores is 275 J/m2. Higher log inactivation occurred at higher doses than 275 J/m2. The typical 254-nm UV-disinfection device can achieve 90% removal.
